content.full_text:
MAY- 9-90 WED 13:24 CITY OF RIVERBANK                           P.02

                                    CITY OF RIVERBANK
                                                                    MAYOR
                                                                    DAVID WHITE

                                                                    VICE MAYOR
                                                                    CARL LEMMONS

6707 THIRD STREET • RIVERBANK, CALIFORNIA 95367 • AREA CODE 209 • 869-3671
                                                                    COUNCILMEN
                                                                    PAUL JOHNSON
                                    FAX TRANSMISSION               CHARLES NEAL
                                                                    SHERMAN PORTER


TO:         JORNATHAN ROTHMORE      FAX # 916 327 2026
            FPFC

FROM:       CHARLES E. NEAL        FAX # 209 869-1177
            COUNCILMEMBER

SUBJECT:    POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST

DATE:       MAY 9, 1990


The Riverbank City Council has been reviewing whether or not concrete
work at the Valley Glen Subdivision in the City of Riverbank is
acceptable to the City of Riverbank. I have a concern about whether or
not I, as a Councilmember, have a conflict and should abstain from
participating in discussing or voting on this matter.

The subdivision is being developed by Hal Porter Homes. Their
subcontractor is Western Stone Products. I was an employee of Western
Stone Products until November of 1989. I worked in construction under
the rules of Operating Engineers Local #3 union.

Approximately 4 years ago, the State law was changed which allowed me
to vote on contracts relating to Western Stone Products, even though I
was an employee at that time. This allowance was predicated on the
fact that while I was an employee, I did not participate or have any
control over decision making or management of the company.

It would appear that my lack of financial interest in Western Stone
Products and the change in the law which allows me to act in Council
matters related to contracts with Western Stone Products, would imply
that I do not have a conflict.

However, just to be on the safe side, I would request that you provide
me with an informal opinion on whether or not you believe I should vote
on the issue of whether or not the City Council should accept the work
performed by Western Stone Products in the Valley Glen Subdivision in
the City of Riverbank.

Time is of the essence since this item comes before the Council for
vote on May 14 at 7:00 P.M. If you can fax your response to me and/or
call me at (209) 869-2430 or (209) 869-3671, I would be very grateful.

Thank you.,

[signature]
CHARLES E. NEAL

The image appears to be a mostly blank or very faded document with minimal visible text. Only scattered dots and punctuation marks are faintly visible throughout the page, but no clearly legible words or coherent text can be discerned.

MAY- 9-90 WED 19:25 CITY OF RIVERBANK                          P.03


                                    CITY OF RIVERBANK
                                                                          MAYOR
                                                                     DAVID WHITE

                                                                     VICE MAYOR
                                                                     CARL LEMMONS
8707 THIRD STREET • RIVERBANK, CALIFORNIA 95367 • AREA CODE 209 • 869-3671
                                                                     COUNCILMEN
                                                                     PAUL JOHNSON
May 9, 1990                                                          CHARLES NEAL
                                                                     SHERMAN PORTER


Mr. Thomas N. Hallinan
Post Office Box 486
Oakdale, California 95361

Subject: VALLEY GLEN SUBDIVISION

Dear Tom:

     It would appear that I might have a conflict of interest under
§87103(C) when Western Stone Products appears before the Council. As
you may recall, the State law was changed a few years ago allowing me
to vote on contracts for Western Stone when I was their employee. This
change was predicated upon the fact that even though I was an employee,
I did not have any financial interest in the company, nor did I have
any decision making authority in the operation and/management of the
company.

     Would you please research the facts for me. Hal Porter Homes are the
prime contractors for this subdivision and Western Stone Products is a
sub-contractor to Hal Porter Homes. The City of Riverbank is dealing
with Hal Porter Homes, rather than Western Stone Products. In the
dealings with this subdivision, as far as a Council decision is
concerned, any decisions made will be between Hal Porter Homes and the
City of Riverbank.

     Under §18702.2, because of the size of Western Stone Products (this
firm performs about $11 million worth or work in this area), and the
size of this contract with Hal Porter Homes (about $500,000), it would
appear that I would not have a conflict and that I could vote on any
aspect of the City's relationship with Hal Porter Homes as the
developer of this subdivision. It would also appear that this coote
relationship to Western Stone Products would also not preclude me from
voting on future Council decisions where the City of Riverbank was not
dealing directly with Western Stone Products; i.e. where they were a
subcontractor of someone else with whom the City was dealing.

     To avoid possible problems, I need to have your response to this
inquiry prior to considering Hal Porter Homes' item Monday night.


                    [signature]
CHARLES E. NEAL
COUNCILMEMBER

[The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with very minimal visible text or markings. Only a few scattered dots or punctuation marks are faintly visible on the page, but they do not form legible words or coherent content.]
